## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
  
  ## Context
  
  Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
  
  ## Scope — Container Source
  
  Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:
  
  - `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
  - `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
  - `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
    - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
    - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
    - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
    - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
    - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
    - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
  - `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
  - `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
  - `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
  
  ## Scope — Tabtargets
  
  Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
  
  - `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
  - `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
  - `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
  - `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
  
  Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
  
  ## Scope — Smoke Test
  
  After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:
  
  1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
  2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
  3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
  4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
  5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
  
  Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
  - **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
  - **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
  - `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
  - Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
  - `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
  - `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
  - `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
  - JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
  - Smoke-test procedure documented.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
  - Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
  - Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
  - Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
  - Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
  
  ## Context
  
  Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
  
  ## Scope — Container Source
  
  Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:
  
  - `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
  - `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
  - `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
    - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
    - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
    - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
    - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
    - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
    - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
  - `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
  - `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
  - `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
  
  ## Scope — Tabtargets
  
  Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
  
  - `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
  - `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
  - `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
  - `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
  
  Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
  
  ## Scope — Smoke Test
  
  After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:
  
  1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
  2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
  3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
  4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
  5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
  
  Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
  - **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
  - **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
  - `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
  - Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
  - `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
  - `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
  - `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
  - JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
  - Smoke-test procedure documented.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
  - Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
  - Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
  - Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
  - Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
25	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
26	- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
27	- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
28	- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
29	- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
30	- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
31	- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
32	- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.
33	
34	## Authoritative Documents
35	
36	| Document | Owns | Path |
37	|----------|------|------|
38	| Application Spec (APCAS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCAS-Specification.md` |
39	| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
40	| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
41	| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

43	# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAZ
44	
45	## Character
46	
47	Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
48	
49	## Prerequisites
50	
51	A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
52	
53	## Context
54	
55	Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
56	
57	## Scope — Container Source
58	
59	Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:
60	
61	- `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
62	- `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
63	- `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
64	  - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
65	  - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
66	  - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
67	  - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
68	  - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
69	  - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
70	- `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
71	- `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
72	- `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
73	
74	## Scope — Tabtargets
75	
76	Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
77	
78	- `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
79	- `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
80	- `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
81	- `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
82	
83	Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
84	
85	## Scope — Smoke Test
86	
87	After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:
88	
89	1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
90	2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
91	3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
92	4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
93	5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
94	
95	Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
96	
97	## Constraints
98	
99	- **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
100	- **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
101	- **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.
102	
103	## Exit Criteria
104	
105	- `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
106	- `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
107	- Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
108	- `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
109	- `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
110	- `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
111	- JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
112	- Smoke-test procedure documented.
113	
114	## Out of Scope
115	
116	- Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
117	- Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
118	- Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
119	- Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
120	- Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A9

## Character

Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.

## Problem

Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.

## Solution

Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation

## Key Design Decisions

- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.

## Authoritative Documents

| Document | Owns | Path |
|----------|------|------|
| Application Spec (APCAS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCAS-Specification.md` |
| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAZ

## Character

Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.

## Prerequisites

A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.

## Context

Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.

## Scope — Container Source

Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:

- `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
- `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
- `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
  - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
  - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
  - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
  - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
  - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
  - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
- `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
- `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
- `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).

## Scope — Tabtargets

Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:

- `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
- `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
- `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
- `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).

Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.

## Scope — Smoke Test

After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:

1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.

Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.

## Constraints

- **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
- **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
- **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
- `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
- Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
- `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
- `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
- `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
- JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
- Smoke-test procedure documented.

## Out of Scope

- Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
- Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
- Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
- Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
- Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

## Mount: ₣A9 apck-1-proof-of-life

**Racing heats** (7 total): A-, A9, A8, A7, A6, Ah, AU — A9 at 18/27 done

**Paddock summary:** Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (Tauri desktop app) — clipboard interceptor for Epic EHR that scrubs PHI before pasting into Open Evidence. Three-tier detection (regex → label → dictionary), no-JS rule, conservative bias, manual quit+relaunch deploy ceremony. Authoritative docs: APCAS, APCPS, claude-context, RCG.

**Recent work on this heat:**
- `₢A9AAV` — APCS0/APCPS restructure around 10-discerner two-placement architecture (specs only)
- `₣A9` — DOCKER_HOST=ssh:// recommendation note for Ann's Mac
- `₢A9AAY` — NSPasteboard FFI for declared-flavor capture

**Heads up — apparent prior officium activity:** Recent git log shows two heat-affiliated commits I didn't make (`53652098` "container-scaffolding", `a74229c4` "rust-container-integration") tagged `₣A9`. The pace ₢A9AAZ is still listed as "rough" and "next" — these may be in-progress scaffolding work from officium 260419-1010 that didn't formally close. I should investigate before writing files to avoid overwriting another officium's work.

**Next pace:** `₢A9AAZ` — container-scaffolding (rough)

Greenfield Python+Docker scaffolding per APCPS Container Architecture: Dockerfile (Python 3.11-slim, transformers+torch-cpu, scispacy, stanza), entrypoint.py polling loop with atomic temp+rename, three discerner modules (stanford/spacy/stanza), four `apcw-c*` tabtargets, smoke test producing valid `{N}.json`.

**Name fit:** "container-scaffolding" matches the docket exactly — no rename needed.

**Proposed approach:**
- Investigate prior officium's commits (53652098, a74229c4) — what's already on disk?
- Reconcile: either build on existing scaffolding or treat docket as source of truth
- Implement remaining gaps: container source files, tabtargets, smoke test, doc
- Verify JSON schema byte-compatibility with APCPS

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — needs judgment for (a) reconciling unexpected prior work, (b) JSON schema matching, (c) tabtarget minting. The docket itself nominates sonnet-scale, but the prior-work surprise warrants opus.

Want me to investigate the prior commits first, then proceed?

message.content: investigate, though I'm skeptical there is anything there that needs reconciling: this container involvement is a bold new direction and we haven't started any implementation.

6d583391 jjb:1011-276d0e9d::i: OFFICIUM 260419-1008
276d0e9d jjb:1011-23e5939d:₢A9AAY:n: NSPasteboard FFI for declared-flavor capture. New apcrb_pasteboard module (prefix apcrb — B for Board, matching the source-module initial-of-noun convention; docket's apcrk/K-for-kernel reconsidered since AppKit is userspace and the letter tracks no obvious noun) exposes apcrb_capture_declared_flavors(dir, classification, index) that reads the general NSPasteboard via objc2-app-kit 0.3.2, enumerates the first item's declared UTIs via NSPasteboardItem::types, and writes each dataForType payload to {N}-in.{tag}.{ext} in the journal directory. UTI-to-extension mapping is data-driven: public.rtf→rtf, public.utf8-plain-text→utf8.txt, public.utf16-plain-text→utf16.txt, public.plain-text→txt, public.html→html, public.{tiff,png,jpeg}→matching, public.{url,file-url}→url/fileurl; anything else (legacy NSPasteboard names included) falls through zapcrb_sanitize_uti to a {sanitized}.bin form where dots/slashes/whitespace/punctuation collapse to single hyphens, leading/trailing hyphens trim, and empty input yields 'flavor'. FFI errors log at the boundary via apcrl_error_now! with uti/filename/byte-count detail before returning Err. Non-macOS arm returns Err('apcrb: NSPasteboard FFI is macOS-only') to preserve cross-platform compilation honestly. apcrh_harvest::apcrh_capture_all_flavors stripped of its fallback arboard path — original docket called for 'arboard as defensive fallback if native returns zero' but load-bearing review flagged it as non-executed-in-practice, platform-dead on non-mac, and actively harmful on mac FFI failure (masks the signal by duplicating work apcap_main's classifier already did with arboard). Now apcrh is a thin wrapper: create dir, scan next index via zapcrh_scan_next_index's leading-digit-run parse, delegate to apcrb, propagate. Dead writers zapcrh_write_text and zapcrh_write_html deleted; std::io::Write and arboard imports in apcrh dropped. apcrh module docstring rewritten to state the visible-failure-beats-silent-duplication contract. Cargo.toml gains macOS-only [target] section pinning objc2=0.6.4, objc2-foundation=0.3.2, objc2-app-kit=0.3.2 with NSPasteboard+NSPasteboardItem features — versions match whatever Tauri already pulls transitively so no second resolution of the objc2 tree. 87 tests pass (+11 new apctb_pasteboard cases covering known public UTIs, image/url UTIs, unknown-UTI sanitization with bin suffix, legacy NSPasteboard names, hyphen collapse and trim, forbidden-filename-char scrubbing, alphanumeric/hyphen passthrough, empty-input placeholder, and property test that every unknown extension is filename-safe); deny(warnings) holds. APCPS spec updates, claude-context refresh, and on-device verification on Ann's MBA pending before wrap.

content.text: Reading APCPS Container Architecture, the existing zipper/workbench/CLI, and the BCG spec in parallel.

content.content:
5:This document specifies the engineering pipeline for the proof-of-life prototype. It covers detection algorithms, tech stack, data sources, wire-format JSON schemas, the container image, project structure, and scope boundaries. It does NOT cover the user experience — see [APCAS-Specification.md](APCAS-Specification.md) for the UX plan — nor the detection pipeline's conceptual vocabulary, which lives in [APCS0-SpecTop.adoc](APCS0-SpecTop.adoc).
20:## Tech Stack
45:| Neural inference | `transformers`, `torch` (CPU-only wheel) | Native PyTorch runtime for `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` |
47:| Biomedical NLP | `scispacy`, `spacy`, model `en_core_sci_md` | Clinical-domain spaCy pipeline — POS, dependency parse, biomedical NER |
48:| General NLP | `stanza` (English UD package) | POS, morphological features, dependency parse, OntoNotes-style NER as a second independent NER signal |
50:`scispacy` is layered on `spacy` and tracks upstream spaCy releases with occasional lag; the Dockerfile pins both to a compatible pair. Torch is installed as the CPU-only wheel explicitly — GPU wheels are unusable inside a Linux container on macOS (Metal/MPS does not pass through) and would only bloat the image.
97:**Path.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — outside any repo tree. The directory is created lazily at application startup (for the log tee) or on first capture (for harvests), whichever comes first. It is simultaneously the bind-mount target for the container. A `.gitignore` entry for `apcjd/` is present as belt-and-suspenders against a misdirected `$HOME` resolution that lands in-tree.
122:[Omitted long matching line]
253:All three run inside `apcs_container` (see Container Architecture below). Each consumes `{N}-in.txt` (the normalized text) and contributes a key to the consolidated `{N}.json`.
257:| `apcs_stanford_scan` | `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` (HuggingFace transformers, PyTorch CPU) | container | PHI named entities with native 8-label taxonomy |
258:| `apcs_spacy_scan` | scispaCy `en_core_sci_md` | container | POS tags, morphological features, dependency parse, biomedical NER |
259:| `apcs_stanza_scan` | Stanza English UD pipeline (default English package) | container | POS (UD + language-specific), morphological features, lemma, dependency parse, OntoNotes-style NER |
261:**Stanford taxonomy.** The `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` model uses a **flat 8-label** taxonomy (no BIO prefix):
282:## Container Architecture
290:Container lifecycle is **managed externally from Clipbuddy** — via tabtargets, not by the Tauri app. Clipbuddy presumes the container is already running; if it isn't, the bind-mount directory simply fails to receive `{N}.json` files and combining runs with an empty container-side evidence contribution (graceful degradation — Rust-side evidence still drives the anonymized output).

It covers detection algorithms, tech stack, data sources, wire-format JSON schemas, the container image, project structure, and scope boundaries. It does NOT cover the user experience — see [APCAS-Specification.md](APCAS-Specification.md) for the UX plan — nor the detection pipeline's conceptual vocabulary, which lives in [APCS0-SpecTop.adoc](APCS0-SpecTop.adoc).
20:## Tech Stack
45:| Neural inference | `transformers`, `torch` (CPU-only wheel) | Native PyTorch runtime for `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` |
47:| Biomedical NLP | `scispacy`, `spacy`, model `en_core_sci_md` | Clinical-domain spaCy pipeline — POS, dependency parse, biomedical NER |
48:| General NLP | `stanza` (English UD package) | POS, morphological features, dependency parse, OntoNotes-style NER as a second independent NER signal |
50:`scispacy` is layered on `spacy` and tracks upstream spaCy releases with occasional lag; the Dockerfile pins both to a compatible pair. Torch is installed as the CPU-only wheel explicitly — GPU wheels are unusable inside a Linux container on macOS (Metal/MPS does not pass through) and would only bloat the image.
97:**Path.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — outside any repo tree. The directory is created lazily at application startup (for the log tee) or on first capture (for harvests), whichever comes first. It is simultaneously the bind-mount target for the container. A `.gitignore` entry for `apcjd/` is present as belt-and-suspenders against a misdirected `$HOME` resolution that lands in-tree.

content.content:
245	| `apcs_city_scan` | cities.txt | ADDRESS | PHI candidate (may remap to FACILITY under combining) — emit on match |
246	| `apcs_english_scan` | english_whitelist.txt | (none) | Suppression evidence — emit on match |
247	| `apcs_medical_scan` | medical_whitelist.txt | (none) | Suppression evidence — emit on match |
248	
249	The five scans, together, replace the single Tier-3 "dictionary scan" of earlier drafts. The whitelist scans (`english`, `medical`) previously existed only as flags on dictionary findings; promoting them to first-class discerners makes their evidence addressable by combining rules on equal footing with the name/location scans.
250	
251	### Container Discerners — parameterization
252	
253	All three run inside `apcs_container` (see Container Architecture below). Each consumes `{N}-in.txt` (the normalized text) and contributes a key to the consolidated `{N}.json`.
254	
255	| Discerner | Model | Placement | Primary output signal |
256	|-----------|-------|-----------|----------------------|
257	| `apcs_stanford_scan` | `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` (HuggingFace transformers, PyTorch CPU) | container | PHI named entities with native 8-label taxonomy |
258	| `apcs_spacy_scan` | scispaCy `en_core_sci_md` | container | POS tags, morphological features, dependency parse, biomedical NER |
259	| `apcs_stanza_scan` | Stanza English UD pipeline (default English package) | container | POS (UD + language-specific), morphological features, lemma, dependency parse, OntoNotes-style NER |
260	
261	**Stanford taxonomy.** The `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` model uses a **flat 8-label** taxonomy (no BIO prefix):
262	
263	```
264	O, VENDOR, DATE, HCW, HOSPITAL, ID, PATIENT, PHONE
265	```
266	
267	Runs of identical labels form a single entity. This taxonomy is read from `config.json:id2label` at container image build time — no hardcoded mapping on the Rust side. Mapping these native labels to `apcs_phi_category` is a combining concern (deferred).
268	
269	**Observed Stanford strengths** (recorded from spike evaluation on APCK synthetic fixtures):
270	- Multi-token person names merged as single spans (`Margaret J. Thornton` → one `PATIENT`)
271	- Facility names merged (`Maine Medical Center` → one `HOSPITAL`)
272	- Narrative-scope PHI catches where positional anchors are absent
273	
274	**Observed Stanford weaknesses:** structured identifiers (SSN, URL, address, device serial) fragment across multiple labels — less reliable than regex for the same categories. The regex scan and Stanford scan are complementary; combining is where the complementarity is cashed in.
275	
276	**Historical spike.** An earlier spike (`apcnsa_main.rs` + formerly APCNS0-NeuralStanford.md) validated Stanford de-identifier utility by running it via ONNX inside a standalone Rust binary. That binary continues to exist as a reference / offline assay tool; the container-based path supersedes it for the production pipeline. The ONNX export ceremony is no longer required — the container uses HuggingFace transformers + PyTorch natively.
277	
278	**spaCy choice.** `en_core_sci_md` is the clinical-domain model from scispaCy. Provides POS tagging, dependency parsing, and biomedical NER. Chosen for its syntactic signals (POS + dependency parse), which are what enable disambiguation of homographs like "may" (modal verb, AUX) vs. "May" (proper noun, PROPN). Its NER is biomedical-focused (diseases, chemicals) and provides supplementary signal; PHI-focused NER is the Stanford scan's job.
279	
280	**Stanza choice.** English Universal Dependencies pipeline. Provides a second, architecturally independent syntactic analyzer plus OntoNotes-style NER (PERSON, ORG, GPE, DATE). The two-parser redundancy is deliberate — when spaCy and Stanza agree that "may" is AUX, that's independent corroboration; when they disagree, combining can weight accordingly. Whether both ship in MVP-to-Ann or whether evaluation narrows to one is a decision for a later pace.
281	
282	## Container Architecture
283	
284	### Purpose
285	
286	Isolate Python-native NLP algorithms from the Rust application process while preserving a strong privacy and security posture. The container's zero-network, capability-dropped, non-root posture means the sophisticated algorithms can be iterated on and scaled up without widening the trust boundary of Clipbuddy itself.
287	
288	### Lifecycle
289	
290	Container lifecycle is **managed externally from Clipbuddy** — via tabtargets, not by the Tauri app. Clipbuddy presumes the container is already running; if it isn't, the bind-mount directory simply fails to receive `{N}.json` files and combining runs with an empty container-side evidence contribution (graceful degradation — Rust-side evidence still drives the anonymized output).
291	
292	Tabtarget set (names in the `apcw-X` family; concrete names pending mint):
293	
294	| Tabtarget | Purpose |
295	|-----------|---------|
296	| Image build | `docker build` the container image from the Dockerfile in the project tree |
297	| Container start | Truncate `container-log.txt`, `docker run` the image with the bind mount and security flags |
298	| Container stop | `docker stop` + `docker rm` |
299	| Container status | Report whether the container is running, which image it's bound to, whether it can see the bind-mount directory |
300	
301	### Bind Mount
302	
303	**Host directory.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — the journal directory, bind-mounted into the container at a container-local path (e.g., `/work/apcjd/`). Single directory, read+write for both sides.
304	
305	**Container files it reads:**
306	- `{N}-in.txt` — normalized text for the input with index `N`
307	
308	**Container files it writes:**
309	- `{N}.json.tmp` — transient, during assembly
310	- `{N}.json` — consolidated findings, published atomically via `rename(2)`
311	- `container-log.txt` — running diagnostic log
312	
313	**Indexing discipline.** The container scans `$HOME/apcjd/` for `{N}-in.txt` files. It processes **only the highest-indexed** input and ignores all others. This implements a drop-old backlog policy: if Ann copies three things quickly, the container processes only the newest; older inputs are skipped. Cleanup of stale inputs and outputs is manual.
314	
315	### Wire Protocol
316	
317	1. Clipbuddy writes `{N}-in.txt` containing `apcs_normalized_text` to `$HOME/apcjd/`.
318	2. The container (in a polling or inotify-driven loop; implementation detail) observes the new file, confirms `N` is the highest index present, and runs all three discerners on its contents.
319	3. The container writes `{N}.json.tmp` with the consolidated findings from all three discerners.
320	4. When all algorithms have completed and the file is closed, the container `rename(2)`s `{N}.json.tmp` to `{N}.json`. POSIX guarantees the rename is atomic within the same filesystem — readers either see the old (absent) state or the final file, never a partially written intermediate.
321	5. Clipbuddy (watching via the `notify` crate) observes `{N}.json` existing, reads and deserializes it, and folds its findings into the evidence pool alongside the Rust-side findings.
322	
323	No sockets, no HTTP, no pipes — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.
324	
325	### Container Output JSON Schema
326	
327	The `{N}.json` file has this top-level shape:
328	
329	```json
330	{
331	  "index": 10000,
332	  "stanford": {
333	    "findings": [ /* see Stanford finding schema */ ]
334	  },
335	  "spacy": {
336	    "findings": [ /* see spaCy finding schema */ ]
337	  },
338	  "stanza": {
339	    "findings": [ /* see Stanza finding schema */ ]
340	  }
341	}
342	```
343	
344	No schema version field for the prototype — the shape is pinned to the container image version. If the schema evolves, the image tag changes, and any Clipbuddy built against an older schema is simply incompatible with the newer image.
345	
346	Per-discerner finding schemas:
347	
348	**Stanford finding:**
349	```json
350	{
351	  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
352	  "start": 45,
353	  "end": 65,
354	  "label": "PATIENT",
355	  "confidence": 0.987
356	}
357	```
358	- `start`/`end` are character offsets into `apcs_normalized_text`.
359	- `label` is one of the 8 native labels (non-`O` values only; `O` tokens are not emitted as findings).
360	- `confidence` is the model's softmax confidence for the dominant label over the span.
361	
362	**spaCy finding.** Two variants share a single discerner namespace; each finding is either token-level or entity-level, distinguished by a `kind` field:
363	
364	```json
365	{
366	  "kind": "token",
367	  "text": "may",
368	  "start": 120,
369	  "end": 123,
370	  "pos": "AUX",
371	  "tag": "MD",
372	  "morph": "VerbType=Mod",
373	  "lemma": "may",
374	  "head": 5,
375	  "dep": "aux"
376	}
377	```
378	
379	```json
380	{
381	  "kind": "entity",
382	  "text": "aspirin",
383	  "start": 84,
384	  "end": 91,
385	  "label": "CHEMICAL"
386	}
387	```
388	
389	**Stanza finding.** Same two-variant pattern:
390	
391	```json
392	{
393	  "kind": "token",
394	  "text": "Margaret",
395	  "start": 45,
396	  "end": 53,
397	  "upos": "PROPN",
398	  "xpos": "NNP",
399	  "feats": "Number=Sing",
400	  "lemma": "Margaret",
401	  "head": 2,
402	  "deprel": "nsubj",
403	  "ner": "B-PERSON"
404	}
405	```
406	
407	```json
408	{
409	  "kind": "entity",
410	  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
411	  "start": 45,
412	  "end": 65,
413	  "label": "PERSON"
414	}
415	```
416	
417	These schemas are illustrative for the prototype — concrete field ordering, optionality, and type precision will be fixed by the Rust deserializer types in `apcd/src/` when the consumer is written.
418	
419	### Security Posture
420	
421	| Control | Setting | Rationale |
422	|---------|---------|-----------|
423	| Network | `--network=none` | The container cannot initiate or accept any network traffic — eliminates PHI exfiltration risk |
424	| Capabilities | `--cap-drop=all` | No Linux capabilities beyond the bare minimum needed to run Python and write to the bind mount |
425	| Filesystem | Read-only root filesystem (`--read-only`), writable bind-mount only | The container can mutate only the bind-mounted directory |
426	| User | Non-root (explicit `USER` directive in Dockerfile) | Standard container hygiene; bind-mount file ownership maps to host UID/GID |
427	| Resource limits | None (prototype scale) | Memory and CPU caps deferred until empirical usage is observed |
428	
429	### Rationale Summary
430	
431	- **Python ecosystem unlocked**: scispaCy, Stanza, HuggingFace transformers run natively without ONNX export ceremony or Rust-Python FFI.
432	- **Combining stays in Rust**: strongly typed deserialization, full test coverage on the combining logic, unchanged language boundary.
433	- **Zero-network container**: PHI cannot leave the container over the network because there is no network. Stronger posture than a host-side Python venv.
434	- **File-I/O wire protocol**: the simplest possible interface — debuggable by eyeballing a directory, no protocol parser to write or maintain.
435	- **External lifecycle**: container up/down is a developer-directed action, decoupled from Clipbuddy startup. Simplifies the prototype's failure modes.
436	
437	## Dictionary Data
438	
439	### Proof-of-Life Sizing
440	
441	Curated-small dictionaries that prove the architecture without requiring full datasets. Each paired with its own scan (see Dictionary Scans above):
442	
443	| Dictionary | Source | License | Size | File |
444	|------------|--------|---------|------|------|
445	| Surnames | US Census 2010, top 1,000 by frequency | Public domain | ~1,000 entries | `dictionaries/surnames.txt` |
446	| First names | SSA baby names, top 1,000 | Public domain | ~1,000 entries | `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` |
447	| Cities | US state capitals + top 100 cities by population | Public domain | ~150 entries | `dictionaries/cities.txt` |
448	| Medical whitelist | Hand-curated: common medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations | Original curation | ~500 entries | `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` |
449	| English whitelist | Top 5,000 common English words | Public domain | ~5,000 entries | `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` |
450	
451	Total: ~8,000 entries, ~80KB on disk.
452	
453	### Full Dataset Sizing (Future)
454	
455	| Dictionary | Source | License | Size |
456	|------------|--------|---------|------|
457	| Surnames | US Census 2010, complete | Public domain | ~160,000 |
458	| First names | SSA baby names, complete | Public domain | ~100,000 |
459	| Cities | US Census incorporated places | Public domain | ~30,000 |
460	| Medical whitelist | SNOMED-CT + RxNorm + LOINC subsets | NLM license (redistribution constraints) | ~100,000+ |
461	| English whitelist | Full dictionary | Public domain | ~170,000 |
462	
463	## Anonymization
464

### Container Discerners — parameterization

All three run inside `apcs_container` (see Container Architecture below). Each consumes `{N}-in.txt` (the normalized text) and contributes a key to the consolidated `{N}.json`.

```

```

Runs of identical labels form a single entity. This taxonomy is read from `config.json:id2label` at container image build time — no hardcoded mapping on the Rust side. Mapping these native labels to `apcs_phi_category` is a combining concern (deferred).

**Observed Stanford strengths** (recorded from spike evaluation on APCK synthetic fixtures):
- Multi-token person names merged as single spans (`Margaret J. Thornton` → one `PATIENT`)
- Facility names merged (`Maine Medical Center` → one `HOSPITAL`)

**Observed Stanford weaknesses:** structured identifiers (SSN, URL, address, device serial) fragment across multiple labels — less reliable than regex for the same categories. The regex scan and Stanford scan are complementary; combining is where the complementarity is cashed in.

**Historical spike.** An earlier spike (`apcnsa_main.rs` + formerly APCNS0-NeuralStanford.md) validated Stanford de-identifier utility by running it via ONNX inside a standalone Rust binary. That binary continues to exist as a reference / offline assay tool; the container-based path supersedes it for the production pipeline. The ONNX export ceremony is no longer required — the container uses HuggingFace transformers + PyTorch natively.

**spaCy choice.** `en_core_sci_md` is the clinical-domain model from scispaCy. Provides POS tagging, dependency parsing, and biomedical NER. Chosen for its syntactic signals (POS + dependency parse), which are what enable disambiguation of homographs like "may" (modal verb, AUX) vs. "May" (proper noun, PROPN). Its NER is biomedical-focused (diseases, chemicals) and provides supplementary signal; PHI-focused NER is the Stanford scan's job.

**Stanza choice.** English Universal Dependencies pipeline. Provides a second, architecturally independent syntactic analyzer plus OntoNotes-style NER (PERSON, ORG, GPE, DATE). Whether both ship in MVP-to-Ann or whether evaluation narrows to one is a decision for a later pace.

## Container Architecture

### Purpose

Isolate Python-native NLP algorithms from the Rust application process while preserving a strong privacy and security posture. The container's zero-network, capability-dropped, non-root posture means the sophisticated algorithms can be iterated on and scaled up without widening the trust boundary of Clipbuddy itself.

### Lifecycle

Container lifecycle is **managed externally from Clipbuddy** — via tabtargets, not by the Tauri app. Clipbuddy presumes the container is already running; if it isn't, the bind-mount directory simply fails to receive `{N}.json` files and combining runs with an empty container-side evidence contribution (graceful degradation — Rust-side evidence still drives the anonymized output).

Tabtarget set (names in the `apcw-X` family; concrete names pending mint):

| Tabtarget | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Image build | `docker build` the container image from the Dockerfile in the project tree |
| Container start | Truncate `container-log.txt`, `docker run` the image with the bind mount and security flags |
| Container stop | `docker stop` + `docker rm` |
| Container status | Report whether the container is running, which image it's bound to, whether it can see the bind-mount directory |

### Bind Mount

**Host directory.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — the journal directory, bind-mounted into the container at a container-local path (e.g., `/work/apcjd/`). Single directory, read+write for both sides.

**Container files it reads:**
- `{N}-in.txt` — normalized text for the input with index `N`

**Container files it writes:**
- `{N}.json.tmp` — transient, during assembly
- `{N}.json` — consolidated findings, published atomically via `rename(2)`
- `container-log.txt` — running diagnostic log

**Indexing discipline.** The container scans `$HOME/apcjd/` for `{N}-in.txt` files. It processes **only the highest-indexed** input and ignores all others. This implements a drop-old backlog policy: if Ann copies three things quickly, the container processes only the newest; older inputs are skipped. Cleanup of stale inputs and outputs is manual.

### Wire Protocol

1. Clipbuddy writes `{N}-in.txt` containing `apcs_normalized_text` to `$HOME/apcjd/`.
2. The container (in a polling or inotify-driven loop; implementation detail) observes the new file, confirms `N` is the highest index present, and runs all three discerners on its contents.
3. The container writes `{N}.json.tmp` with the consolidated findings from all three discerners.
4. When all algorithms have completed and the file is closed, the container `rename(2)`s `{N}.json.tmp` to `{N}.json`. POSIX guarantees the rename is atomic within the same filesystem — readers either see the old (absent) state or the final file, never a partially written intermediate.
5. Clipbuddy (watching via the `notify` crate) observes `{N}.json` existing, reads and deserializes it, and folds its findings into the evidence pool alongside the Rust-side findings.

No sockets, no HTTP, no pipes — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.

### Container Output JSON Schema

The `{N}.json` file has this top-level shape:

```json
{
  "index": 10000,
  "stanford": {
    "findings": [ /* see Stanford finding schema */ ]
  },
  "spacy": {
    "findings": [ /* see spaCy finding schema */ ]
  },
  "stanza": {

  }
}
```

No schema version field for the prototype — the shape is pinned to the container image version. If the schema evolves, the image tag changes, and any Clipbuddy built against an older schema is simply incompatible with the newer image.

Per-discerner finding schemas:

**Stanford finding:**

  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",

  "end": 65,

```

- `confidence` is the model's softmax confidence for the dominant label over the span.

**spaCy finding.** Two variants share a single discerner namespace; each finding is either token-level or entity-level, distinguished by a `kind` field:

{

  "end": 123,
  "pos": "AUX",
  "tag": "MD",
  "morph": "VerbType=Mod",
  "lemma": "may",
  "head": 5,
  "dep": "aux"
}
```

```json
{
  "kind": "entity",
  "text": "aspirin",
  "start": 84,
  "end": 91,
  "label": "CHEMICAL"
}
```

**Stanza finding.** Same two-variant pattern:

```json
{
  "kind": "token",
  "text": "Margaret",
  "start": 45,
  "end": 53,
  "upos": "PROPN",
  "xpos": "NNP",
  "feats": "Number=Sing",
  "lemma": "Margaret",
  "head": 2,
  "deprel": "nsubj",
  "ner": "B-PERSON"
}
```

```json
{
  "kind": "entity",
  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
  "start": 45,
  "end": 65,
  "label": "PERSON"
}
```

These schemas are illustrative for the prototype — concrete field ordering, optionality, and type precision will be fixed by the Rust deserializer types in `apcd/src/` when the consumer is written.

### Security Posture

| Control | Setting | Rationale |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| Network | `--network=none` | The container cannot initiate or accept any network traffic — eliminates PHI exfiltration risk |
| Capabilities | `--cap-drop=all` | No Linux capabilities beyond the bare minimum needed to run Python and write to the bind mount |
| Filesystem | Read-only root filesystem (`--read-only`), writable bind-mount only | The container can mutate only the bind-mounted directory |
| User | Non-root (explicit `USER` directive in Dockerfile) | Standard container hygiene; bind-mount file ownership maps to host UID/GID |
| Resource limits | None (prototype scale) | Memory and CPU caps deferred until empirical usage is observed |

### Rationale Summary

- **Python ecosystem unlocked**: scispaCy, Stanza, HuggingFace transformers run natively without ONNX export ceremony or Rust-Python FFI.
- **Combining stays in Rust**: strongly typed deserialization, full test coverage on the combining logic, unchanged language boundary.
- **Zero-network container**: PHI cannot leave the container over the network because there is no network. Stronger posture than a host-side Python venv.
- **File-I/O wire protocol**: the simplest possible interface — debuggable by eyeballing a directory, no protocol parser to write or maintain.
- **External lifecycle**: container up/down is a developer-directed action, decoupled from Clipbuddy startup. Simplifies the prototype's failure modes.

## Dictionary Data

### Proof-of-Life Sizing

Curated-small dictionaries that prove the architecture without requiring full datasets. Each paired with its own scan (see Dictionary Scans above):

| Dictionary | Source | License | Size | File |
|------------|--------|---------|------|------|
| Surnames | US Census 2010, top 1,000 by frequency | Public domain | ~1,000 entries | `dictionaries/surnames.txt` |
| First names | SSA baby names, top 1,000 | Public domain | ~1,000 entries | `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` |
| Cities | US state capitals + top 100 cities by population | Public domain | ~150 entries | `dictionaries/cities.txt` |
| Medical whitelist | Hand-curated: common medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations | Original curation | ~500 entries | `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` |
| English whitelist | Top 5,000 common English words | Public domain | ~5,000 entries | `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` |

Total: ~8,000 entries, ~80KB on disk.

### Full Dataset Sizing (Future)

| Dictionary | Source | License | Size |
|------------|--------|---------|------|
| Surnames | US Census 2010, complete | Public domain | ~160,000 |
| First names | SSA baby names, complete | Public domain | ~100,000 |
| Cities | US Census incorporated places | Public domain | ~30,000 |
| Medical whitelist | SNOMED-CT + RxNorm + LOINC subsets | NLM license (redistribution constraints) | ~100,000+ |
| English whitelist | Full dictionary | Public domain | ~170,000 |

## Anonymization

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCZ Zipper - Colophon registry for APCK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZAPCZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZAPCZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zapcz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "apcz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled
  zbuz_sentinel

  # Build — Tauri build and development (apcw-)
  buz_group APCZ__GROUP_BUILD   "apcw-"   "Build — Tauri build and development"
  buz_enroll APCZ_BUILD          "apcw-b"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_build"         ""  "cargo tauri build (release)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_RUN            "apcw-r"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_run"           ""  "cargo tauri dev (local development)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DEPLOY         "apcw-D"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_deploy"        ""  "Build + scp to anns-macbook-air"
  buz_enroll APCZ_FIXTURE_LOAD   "apcw-fl" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_fixture_load"  "imprint"  "Run apcal to load fixture HTML onto clipboard"
  buz_enroll APCZ_TEST           "apcw-t"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_test"          ""  "cargo test in apcd/"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DICT_REFRESH  "apcw-dr" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_dictionary_refresh" "" "Refresh dictionaries from public sources"
  buz_enroll APCZ_BATCH_ASSAY  "apcw-ba" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_batch_assay"       "param1" "Batch assay — run detection pipeline on HTML directory"
  buz_enroll APCZ_NS_INSTALL   "apcw-nsi" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_neural_stanford_install" "" "Neural Stanford install — convergent venv + ONNX export (always reaches a working state)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_NS_ASSAY     "apcw-nsa" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_neural_stanford_assay"  "param1" "Neural Stanford assay — run apcnsa on HTML directory"

  readonly ZAPCZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zapcz_healthcheck() {
  zapcz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zapcz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module apcz not kindled - call zapcz_kindle first"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCW Workbench - Routes APCK commands to CLIs via zipper registry
#
# All commands dispatch via buz_exec_lookup (see apcz_zipper.sh for colophon mapping).

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory
APCW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
source "${APCW_SCRIPT_DIR}/apcz_zipper.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Kindle dispatch and zipper registry
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zapcz_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
apcw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "APCWSHOW: $*"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

apcw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  apcw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel
  zapcz_healthcheck

  apcw_show "BURD environment verified"

  buz_exec_lookup "${z_command}" "${APCW_SCRIPT_DIR}" "$@"
}

apcw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  apcw_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

apcw_main "$@"

55	    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
56	  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"

58	  local -r z_bundle_dir="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/target/release/bundle/macos"

61	  local -r z_ssh_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_ssh_stderr.txt"
62	  local -r z_scp_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_scp_stderr.txt"
63	
64	  # Find first .app bundle via glob

66	  for z_app_name in "${z_bundle_dir}"/*.app; do
67	    test -d "${z_app_name}" && break
68	    z_app_name=""
69	  done
70	  test -n "${z_app_name}" || buc_die "No .app bundle found in ${z_bundle_dir}"
71	
72	  # Measure bundle size via temp file
73	  du -sh "${z_app_name}" > "${z_size_file}" 2>"${z_size_stderr}" \
74	    || buc_die "Failed to measure bundle — see ${z_size_stderr}"
75	  local -r z_size_raw=$(<"${z_size_file}")
76	  test -n "${z_size_raw}" || buc_die "Empty size output from ${z_size_file}"
77	  local -r z_bundle_size="${z_size_raw%%$'\t'*}"
78	
79	  buc_step "Deploying to anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
80	  # find (not rm *.app) because Ann's remote shell is zsh, which errors on

82	  ssh anns-macbook-air 'mkdir -p /Users/Shared/apcua && find /Users/Shared/apcua -maxdepth 1 -name "*.app" -exec rm -rf {} +' 2>"${z_ssh_stderr}" \
83	    || buc_die "Failed to clean staging dir — see ${z_ssh_stderr}"
84	  scp -r "${z_app_name}" anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/ 2>"${z_scp_stderr}" \
85	    || buc_die "Failed to deploy bundle — see ${z_scp_stderr}"
86	  buc_step "Deploy complete: ${z_app_name##*/} (${z_bundle_size}) → anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
87	
88	  local -r z_app_basename="${z_app_name##*/}"
89	  printf '%s\n' '' \

96	                "  2. In Finder: Go → Go to Folder… → /Users/Shared/apcua/" \
97	                "     Double-click ${z_app_basename}" \
98	                '' \

123	  cargo test --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
124	    > "${z_output_file}" 2>&1 \
125	    || { cat "${z_output_file}"; buc_die "cargo test failed"; }
126	  cat "${z_output_file}"

128	  buc_step "Tests complete: ${z_total_passed} passed"

131	apcc_dictionary_refresh() {

139	  buc_step "Running batch assay on ${z_folio}"
140	  cargo run --bin apcab --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" -- "${z_folio}" \
141	    || buc_die "cargo run apcab failed"
142	}

145	# Neural Stanford spike
146	
147	apcc_neural_stanford_install() {
148	  command -v python3 >/dev/null \
149	    || buc_die "python3 not found on PATH — required for optimum-cli export"
150	
151	  local -r z_model_dir="${ZAPCC_STANFORD_DIR}"
152	  local -r z_venv_dir="${ZAPCC_STANFORD_VENV}"
153	  local -r z_venv_python="${z_venv_dir}/bin/python"
154	  local -r z_optimum_cli="${z_venv_dir}/bin/optimum-cli"
155	
156	  mkdir -p "${z_model_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_model_dir}"
157	
158	  if [[ ! -x "${z_venv_python}" ]]; then
159	    buc_step "Creating venv: ${z_venv_dir}"
160	    python3 -m venv "${z_venv_dir}" \
161	      || buc_die "python3 -m venv ${z_venv_dir} failed"
162	  fi
163	
164	  # optimum 2.x moved ONNX export into a separate package (optimum-onnx).
165	  # Check for the onnx subcommand directly — presence of optimum-cli alone is insufficient.
166	  local z_onnx_registered=0
167	  if [[ -x "${z_optimum_cli}" ]]; then
168	    "${z_optimum_cli}" export --help 2>&1 | grep -q '^\s*onnx\b' && z_onnx_registered=1
169	  fi
170	  if [[ "${z_onnx_registered}" != "1" ]]; then
171	    buc_step "Installing optimum + optimum-onnx into venv"
172	    "${z_venv_python}" -m pip install --upgrade pip \
173	      || buc_die "pip upgrade failed"
174	    "${z_venv_python}" -m pip install optimum optimum-onnx onnxruntime \
175	      || buc_die "pip install optimum optimum-onnx onnxruntime failed"
176	  fi

178	  # Convergent install: clear any prior model artifacts (preserving the venv)
179	  # and re-export from scratch. Each invocation yields a fresh, valid model dir.
180	  # The HuggingFace cache at ~/.cache/huggingface/ keeps the weight download fast
181	  # on repeat runs; the ONNX trace is the only re-computed step.
182	  buc_step "Clearing prior model artifacts from ${z_model_dir}"
183	  find "${z_model_dir}" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name '.venv' -exec rm -rf {} + \
184	    || buc_die "Failed to clear ${z_model_dir}"
185	
186	  buc_step "Exporting ${ZAPCC_STANFORD_MODEL_ID} to ${z_model_dir}"
187	  "${z_optimum_cli}" export onnx \
188	      --model "${ZAPCC_STANFORD_MODEL_ID}" \
189	      --task token-classification \
190	      "${z_model_dir}" \
191	    || buc_die "optimum-cli export onnx failed"

193	  buc_step "Install complete"
194	  ls -la "${z_model_dir}" || true
195	}

201	    test -f "${z_model_dir}/${z_file}" \
202	      || buc_die "Missing ${z_model_dir}/${z_file} — run apcw-nsi first"
203	  done
